name: Route 66 Pawn & Gun
description_short: Route 66 Pawn & Guns is a federally licensed pawnbroker in Mesa, AZ, offering collateral-based loans on firearms and other items with an active ATF license.
description_long:
Route 66 Pawn & Guns operates as a licensed firearms pawnbroker under Federal Firearms License (FFL) number 9-86-013-02-7J-12798, issued to the legal entity 4 NOW SOLUTIONS LLC. The business is located at 1734 E Main St Suite 7 in Mesa, Arizona, and is regulated by the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF). Their FFL license is current and valid through September 1, 2027, classifying them specifically as a "Pawnbroker in Firearms Other Than Destructive Devices" under ATF Type 02 licensing. The business maintains contact availability at 480-464-4444 for inquiries and transactions.

As a federally licensed firearms pawnbroker, Route 66 Pawn & Guns provides collateral-based lending services. Customers can obtain short-term loans by pledging firearms or other personal property as collateral. The business operates within strict federal regulatory frameworks established by the ATF, which requires compliance with all firearms transfer protocols, background check requirements, and record-keeping standards. Their licensing status indicates they are authorized to conduct firearms transactions in accordance with 27 CFR 478.94 and related federal statutes.

Route 66 Pawn & Guns distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on firearms transactions combined with traditional pawnbroking services. As an FFL-licensed dealer, they meet rigorous federal standards for firearms handling and customer verification. The company's current license status and registered business address provide transparency and legitimacy for consumers seeking collateral-based lending. Their presence in the Mesa market provides a local resource for individuals needing short-term liquidity solutions.

The primary caveat for consumers is that pawnbroking inherently involves high-cost short-term lending compared to traditional banking products. Customers risk losing pledged collateral if loans are not repaid, and the terms typically involve significant fees and interest charges. Additionally, firearms transactions carry legal restrictions and background check requirements that may limit eligibility for some borrowers. This business model is best suited for consumers with immediate liquidity needs and valuable items to pledge, rather than those seeking affordable credit alternatives.

diagnosis: Route 66 Pawn & Guns is best for firearms owners or individuals with valuable collateral who need immediate short-term loans and can accept the high costs inherent to pawnbroking. The main caveat is that this is an expensive, short-term lending product with the risk of losing pledged items, and firearms transactions carry federal legal restrictions that may affect eligibility.
